Hi Melanie,

Following our recent discussion, I have put together a revised structure for the Mini Monstar sessions at your center. As you mentioned that some rooms currently have lower attendance, combining these groups will allow you to continue providing the sessions the children love while significantly reducing your overall costs.

For your reference, your current schedule includes two Nursery sessions, one Junior Toddler session, one Toddler session, one Senior Toddler session, and two Kinder sessions, totaling 2.5 hours of session time.

To optimize your budget, I suggest moving to the following streamlined schedule:

- One combined Nursery session (10 minutes total for 11 children)
- One combined Toddler and Junior Toddler session (20 minutes total for 15 children)
- Two Kindy sessions (30 minutes each for up to 22 children per class)

This new arrangement reduces the total time to 1.5 hours, providing a much more cost-effective solution at $260 + GST. Additionally, as we value our partnership, we are happy to provide a further discount to bring the price down to $240 + GST per visit.

Please let me know during the four-session cancellation period if you wish to continue with this new fortnightly schedule starting 22 May. Alternatively, if you prefer not to proceed, the end of this period would mark our final session.

Please let me know if these changes work for your team. Once confirmed, I will update our schedule and provide the adjusted invoice accordingly.

Kind Regards
Katie

Dear  Kaitl

We are incredibly excited to be running our program within your centre.

To ensure smooth communication moving forward, please note that our accounts team (accounts@minimonstars.com) will handle all invoicing and financial matters. For all other items, including scheduling and session feedback, I will be your primary point of contact.

Regarding our upcoming schedule, please see the confirmed session details below:
 Oak on South Early Learning

Commencing-31 March 2026 10:00 AM - 31 March 2026 11:00 AM
Day-Tuesdays

Time-10:00 AM-11:00 AM
Coaches: Sarahpheinna Woulf
Repeats: Fortnightly
Session Type-Gymnastic
Breakdown-2 x 30min Kindy 

We would love for the parents at your centre to stay connected with us, as we offer much more than just our standard incursions. If they are interested in learning more about our program, they can sign up for our email list here: https://www.minimonstars.net/contact.

Additionally, we have developed a dedicated app where children can continue their learning at home or engage in fun sessions during the day. You can find more information about the app here: https://www.minimonstars.net/mini-monstars-app.

As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.

Saraphina's WWC Blue Card Below​
image.png​

Kind Regards

Kind Regards

I am writing to let you know that we unfortunately need to delay our Mini Monstars start date.

I will send the updated coach details once I organize our schedule. Thank you for your understanding and apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Good Afternoon Kim &Tracy, 
Thank you for this morning's session at your centre! Hope you're having a good day.
My name is Jessica, and I’m your Minimonstars coach and main point of contact for the Mackay region. I just wanted to formally introduce myself and express how excited I am to be working with your centre. I absolutely love what I do and am really passionate about creating fun, engaging sessions that the children look forward to every time. I’m also really keen to build strong, positive relationships and make everything feel easy, smooth, and enjoyable on your end.
If you ever have any questions, feedback, or just want to touch base, please don’t hesitate to reach out. I’m always happy to help and support where I can. 
Regarding your weekly sessions, I am now available earlier on Tuesdays. Please let me know if you would like to start our sessions earlier as I have the capacity. 
You may also hear from other members of the Minimonstars team from time to time. They’re simply there to provide additional support and ensure everything is running well for you and your centre.
I’m really looking forward to getting to know everyone better and being part of your centre!
Warm regards,
Jess

Offhand, I’m not impressed that the teacher was late again – arrived 9:40am. While I understand that last week she was still looking for the Centre, but today nothing has changed. We try to get the children organised to be ready for the activity before 9:30am, but this does not seem to matter as your teacher is late.

patterns.prevention:
• Audit all coach schedules this week to identify coverage gaps 2+ weeks in advance and cross-train backup coaches at each centre.

• Contact centres with multiple missed sessions to confirm coach consistency expectations and resolve scheduling conflicts before next term.

• Implement a weekly coach-centre matching report so managers can spot inconsistency patterns early and reassign coaches if needed.

• Survey cancelled families about coach changes as a primary reason for leaving and flag centres losing the same coach repeatedly for immediate intervention.

patterns.prevention:
• Conduct immediate coaching quality audits at all centres, rating coaches on engagement, energy, and attendance—flag any scoring below standard for retraining or replacement.

• Establish backup coach protocols so centres never lose programming due to coach absence; assign secondary coaches to high-risk locations within 48 hours.

• Schedule centre manager calls this week to gather feedback on current coach performance and identify dissatisfaction before cancellations occur.

• Review equipment and program difficulty at centres with low bookings; implement equipment additions or curriculum adjustments within 10 days where needed.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres at the 6-week and 12-week marks to confirm program satisfaction and address any scheduling conflicts before they lead to cancellations.

• Review pricing and contract terms with centres showing budget concerns, and clarify flexibility options around session timing to reduce "fit" issues.

• Schedule quarterly check-ins with centre managers to discuss their capacity for in-house programs and identify opportunities to expand our offering rather than lose them to internal solutions.

• Implement a 60-day advance notice requirement in new contracts and establish an exit interview process to capture switching reasons before centres commit elsewhere.

centres.name: Papilio Early Learning Reedy Creek
centres.closedDesc:
Affinity  Group -direction to cut all incursions immediately at center as already over budget. waived 4 session cancellation to assist with confirmation in writing we will be preferred provider as soon as incursions are back email confirmation

Date Started: 28/11/2025
Number of Sessions Completed: 22
Sessions Missed: 0
Rescheduled: 0

Coaches: Mataus ,Patsy

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget

strong feedback regarding the value of the program and Coach Charlotte's development over time. However, there were several opportunities where we could have strengthened the relationship further. Early in the partnership, feedback received on 20 January 2026 indicated that sessions were initially "rocky," with concerns around coach engagement, one-on-one interaction with children, and communication with educators. While subsequent feedback in February and March showed significant improvement and increasing satisfaction with Charlotte, earlier intervention, coaching support, and follow-up observations may have accelerated this progress and strengthened confidence sooner. Throughout the partnership, obtaining detailed educator feedback proved difficult, with several follow-up attempts resulting in limited information from centre leadership. Building stronger direct relationships with room leaders and educators may have provided richer feedback and allowed us to identify opportunities earlier. There were also ongoing invoicing and procurement challenges between February and April, creating unnecessary administrative friction for the centre. More proactive collaboration with Affinity processes and earlier support from accounts may have simplified this experience. Communication challenges were also evident, with unsuccessful phone contact attempts occurring on several occasions, suggesting alternative communication methods or multiple contact points within the centre could have improved responsiveness. Despite these areas for improvement, the centre consistently expressed that they loved the sessions and that the eventual cancellation was solely the result of a head office decision to remove incursion budgets across centres rather than dissatisfaction with Mini Monstars. The positive relationship was maintained throughout the cancellation process, and the centre specifically invited future discussions in the new financial year. Going forward, earlier coach support, stronger educator-level engagement, streamlined invoicing assistance, and more consistent communication pathways could further strengthen similar centre relationships and improve retention opportunities where external budget pressures arise.

While the center expressed high satisfaction throughout 2025 and early 2026, several factors likely contributed to the decision to cancel, including staffing instability following Miles going MIA with no notice and dealing with a very angry Nicola the friday afternoon  the sudden resignation of Coach Brendan in March 2026 and a period of multiple coach changes that created significant disruption. centres.name: First Early Learning - Frankston South
centres.closedDesc:
Reason for finishing: Have been told by management to stop all incursions, have also been forced stop their music incursions as well, will be doing them in house. Received similar feedback from other FEL

Started: 4th of Feb
Sessions Completed: 11
Sessions Missed: 1
Rescheduled: 3

Coaches:
Marcel (8 Sessions)
Zac (1)
Matthew (1)
Dave (1)

Summary of their time with us:
After a rough start due to a couple early reschedules and not loving coach Zac, feedback was only positive with marcel locked in as their preferred coach. Happy with Dave and Matthew when they were required to fill in again. 

Next point of contact:
Calling late 2026 hoping FEL changes their no incursion stance.
signals: AI reasoning: Centre stopped all external incursions and moved programs in-house. Clean feedback history, no recent issues, positive tone.

preCloseStats.tone_reason: Matthew's performance was fantastic and the centre is resuming the program without hesitation.
